title: SAGE-Kit Ready Resolution Input v1
description: A bounded, language-neutral snapshot presented to a future pure resolver. The entire input instance is admitted only when its deterministic canonical strict JSON representation is at most 16777216 bytes, measured with the complete canonical_byte_budgets algorithm in contract.json: UTF-8 without BOM, object keys lexicographically sorted by Unicode code point sequence, no insignificant whitespace, shortest decimal integers, fixed necessary JSON string escaping, and all other Unicode scalars encoded directly as UTF-8 rather than ASCII escape. Mandatory representation escaping never rewrites identity. This aggregate measurement is admission only, not semantic identity or authority. An individual opaque identity is never rewritten or truncated. Input over this limit makes an optional resolver invocation return a bounded INPUT_TOO_LARGE Error and never a partial Result; the byte budget does not alter the Graph digest and does not shrink the Graph Contract itself. Strict schema validation, rejection of unknown fields, identity uniqueness checks, and semantic normalization must all succeed before an input_digest can be produced. Schema presence does not activate Graph execution, execute nodes, poll or acquire resources, open a human gate, create a scheduler, create runtime state, mutate the Graph, or grant execution or transition authority. The host supplies already-observed facts and retains execution ownership.

schema_id.description: Stable identity of the Ready Resolution Input v1 boundary.

schema_version.description: Version of this language-neutral input boundary.

graph_digest.description: Stage 2B canonical semantic digest of the immutable Graph snapshot. Resource integrity digests are not execution identity.

graph_generation.description: Observed Graph generation. It grants no mutation, scheduling, gate, or execution authority.

node_states.description: Bounded Runtime State v1-compatible node facts. Each node_id may appear at most once. Duplicate or conflicting records for one node_id are invalid required input and must yield REQUIRED_INPUT_INVALID rather than selection, merging, majority, or a default.

resource_availability.description: Bounded host-observed resource snapshots. Each resource_id may appear at most once. Duplicate or conflicting records for one resource_id are invalid required input and must yield REQUIRED_INPUT_INVALID rather than selection, merging, majority, or a default. A resource omitted from this array must not be inferred to be AVAILABLE.

external_join_decisions.description: Explicit host-supplied decisions for manual-gate and corrective-join boundaries. Each join_id may appear at most once. Duplicate or conflicting records for one join_id are invalid required input and must yield REQUIRED_INPUT_INVALID rather than selection, merging, majority, or a default. Omission never implies satisfaction.

node_id.description: Opaque non-empty Graph node semantic identity. Even when its text looks like a path, it is preserved exactly and is never path-normalized, rejected as a path, treated as file access, or used for a filesystem operation.

graph_id.description: Opaque non-empty Graph resource or join semantic identity. Even when its text looks like a path, it is preserved exactly and is never path-normalized, rejected as a path, treated as file access, or used for a filesystem operation.

reference.description: Bounded repository-neutral evidence or authority reference. Absolute paths, UNC or rooted paths, file URIs, HTTP(S) URIs, and newline-bearing values are invalid.

availability_reason_code.description: Stable machine-readable reason paired deterministically with the availability value.

node_id.description: Opaque non-empty Graph Contract v1 node identity, preserved exactly without an ASCII grammar or 256-character restriction.

status.description: Existing Graph/Runtime node status only; no new runtime status is introduced. SUCCEEDED is a node outcome, not Project Manager acceptance. NO_ACTION_REQUIRED remains evidence-bound and distinct from SUCCEEDED, PASS, or WAIVED. DONE_WITH_CONCERNS does not satisfy required success. HANDOFF is a nonterminal workflow outcome. BLOCKED cannot be bypassed by majority or optional policy.

result_digest.description: Conditional bounded reference to an externally defined Node Result; this contract defines no result digest algorithm.

evidence_refs.description: Bounded repository-neutral evidence references, never embedded evidence, prose authority, output, or transcripts.

resource_snapshot.description: An AVAILABLE value is only a host-observed snapshot and does not mean lock acquisition. BUSY maps to WAITING_RESOURCE, not STOP or BLOCKED. UNAVAILABLE and UNKNOWN remain distinct. Missing resources are not AVAILABLE. This contract does not poll, wait for, or acquire a resource.

resource_id.description: Opaque non-empty Graph Contract v1 resource identity, preserved exactly without an ASCII grammar or 256-character restriction.

availability.description: Explicit host-observed availability. No value performs acquisition or waiting.

evidence_refs.description: Bounded repository-neutral references supporting the observed snapshot.

external_join_decision.description: A host-supplied already-authorized evidence boundary, not a weak gate-open flag. The contract does not validate human identity, does not open a gate, and does not grant authority. A resolver must not infer SATISFIED from filenames, prose, historical state, omission, or defaults. Missing explicit manual-gate or corrective-join decisions fail closed as REQUIRES_EXTERNAL_DECISION. Automatic join policy remains independently binding.

join_id.description: Opaque non-empty Graph Contract v1 join identity, preserved exactly without an ASCII grammar or 256-character restriction.

decision.description: Explicit external decision. PENDING is never approval.

authority_ref.description: Repository-neutral reference to existing authority; it neither creates nor verifies authority.

evidence_refs.description: Bounded repository-neutral evidence references for a satisfied or rejected decision.
